skill.name: diagnose
skill.description: Guides a disciplined debugging loop for difficult bugs and performance regressions, from reproduction through hypothesis testing and regression tests.
skill.summary: Use reproducible feedback loops to diagnose bugs before changing code.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and no malicious behavior was found. The hardcoded URL is localhost-only, the backtick detections are Markdown command examples, and the weak-crypto detections are false positives from ordinary prose. The skill includes normal debugging risks because it may run local scripts, tests, browser tools, and shell commands in the user's project.

low_findings.title: Local Debugging Commands Require User Trust
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill advises using curl, CLI invocations, browser automation, git bisect, timing tools, and grep during debugging. These are legitimate for a diagnosis skill, but users should review commands before running them in sensitive repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and semantically aligned with debugging. I found no user-input command construction, obfuscation, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL points to http://localhost:3000 inside a human-in-the-loop reproduction template. It does not contact an external service or transmit collected data over the network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is limited to localhost and appears only as a user instruction. The script later prints captured values locally and contains no network request command.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The script asks whether clicking an Export button produced an error. This collects reproduction feedback from the user, not system information or host reconnaissance data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The captured value is a yes-or-no symptom report. There is no command that enumerates files, users, processes, environment variables, or network configuration.

low_findings.title: Backtick Command Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported backticks are Markdown examples or formatting. They mention tools such as git bisect, performance.now, grep, a script path, and debug tags, but no executable code invokes a shell from the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is instructional Markdown, not a Ruby or shell program. The examples are contextually appropriate debugging guidance, although command execution remains a normal user-controlled risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptography locations contain skill metadata and debugging checklist text. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API use appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain prose about debugging. I found no cryptographic primitive, hashing algorithm, or security-sensitive encoding at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Human-In-The-Loop Bash Template
dangerous_patterns.description: The repository contains a Bash template that prompts the user and prints captured answers. It is simple and local, but users should inspect edited copies before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The executable script is present and uses standard shell input handling. It does not run dynamic commands or send data externally.

dangerous_patterns.title: Instructional External Command Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends command-line debugging workflows. This is expected for the stated purpose, but commands should remain user-visible and scoped to the target project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command references are direct and understandable. I found no concealed command execution, encoded payloads, or prompt injection text.

content.value_statement: Hard bugs waste time when teams guess before they can reproduce the failure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a disciplined diagnostic loop.
actual_capabilities: Builds a fast pass-or-fail feedback loop before investigating a bug.
Guides reproduction, minimization, hypothesis ranking, instrumentation, and cleanup.
Supports performance regression work with baseline measurements and bisection.
Encourages regression tests at the correct behavioral boundary.
Provides a Bash template for human-in-the-loop reproduction steps.
limitations: It does not automatically fix bugs without project access and a working feedback loop.
It may need user-provided logs, traces, recordings, or environment access.
It relies on local project tools such as tests, browsers, profilers, or CLI commands.
It cannot guarantee a regression test when the codebase has no suitable test boundary.

use_cases.title: Reproduce a Production Bug
use_cases.description: Turn a reported failure into a local loop, capture the exact symptom, and confirm the fix against the original scenario.

use_cases.title: Investigate a Performance Regression
use_cases.description: Measure a baseline, compare versions or configurations, and use bisection before applying a performance fix.

use_cases.title: Debug a Flaky Test or UI Flow
use_cases.description: Raise the reproduction rate, add targeted probes, and remove temporary instrumentation after the cause is confirmed.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Reported Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this bug. First help me build a reproducible feedback loop, then rank hypotheses before changing code.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Minimal Reproduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the diagnose workflow to minimize this failure. Identify the smallest test, CLI input, or browser script that reproduces it.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a Performance Regression
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this performance regression. Establish a baseline, compare likely causes, and propose instrumentation that tests one hypothesis at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Post-Fix Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the complete diagnose workflow. Verify the original repro, add the right regression test, remove instrumentation, and summarize the confirmed cause.

output_examples.input: The export button sometimes fails in staging.
output: A proposed feedback loop using a repeated browser action and captured console output.
A ranked list of likely causes with one falsifiable prediction for each.
A cleanup checklist covering debug logs, prototypes, and regression coverage.

output_examples.input: The API endpoint became slower after the last release.
output: A baseline measurement plan with the same request shape across versions.
A bisection strategy that compares old and new behavior.
A fix verification step that reruns the original performance loop.

best_practices: Start with the fastest deterministic loop that proves the reported symptom.
Rank several falsifiable hypotheses before adding instrumentation or making changes.
Convert the confirmed reproduction into a regression test when the codebase has a correct boundary.
anti_patterns: Do not guess at a fix before reproducing the failure.
Do not add broad logging that is unrelated to a specific prediction.
Do not leave temporary probes, scripts, or debug output in production paths.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant through a structured debugging process based on reproduction, hypotheses, instrumentation, fixes, and regression tests.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill gives command-oriented debugging guidance, but commands should remain visible and approved by the user or project workflow.

faq.question: Can it diagnose flaky bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on increasing the reproduction rate before trying to isolate the cause.

faq.question: Can it handle performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends baseline measurements, profiling, and bisection before changing performance-sensitive code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the symptom, expected behavior, recent changes, logs, traces, recordings, and any steps that reproduce the issue.

faq.question: Is this safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It can be used privately, but users should review local scripts and commands before running them on sensitive code or data.
